openFindings.title: Command-shaped key_files still survive sanitization and are re-emitted as entity paths
openFindings.description: `keepKeyFile()` rejects several command prefixes, but it still accepts `cd ... && npx vitest ...` strings because they look path-like, and `deriveEntities()` then seeds those leaked values straight back into `entities.path`.

openFindings.title: Backfill verification still encodes the pre-refactor active-only default
openFindings.description: The shipped backfill script is inclusive by default, but the dedicated regression suite and phase 004 packet docs still assert that archived and future packets are skipped unless explicitly included, so the verification lane now fails against current behavior.

openFindings.title: The 003 packet lineage is not actually closed out or validator-clean
openFindings.description: Root 003 still declares Level 3 while missing required docs and failing strict validation, phases 001-003 keep open checklist items, and phase 004 is missing `plan.md` and `checklist.md`, so the packet tree does not satisfy the requested “tasks complete, checklists verified” state.

openFindings.title: Canonical packet-doc preference can point entities at an unrelated packet
openFindings.description: The canonical-doc heuristic treats any path that ends with `/spec.md` or similar as canonical, so the root 003 metadata now maps its `spec.md` entity to `00--ai-systems/001-global-shared/spec.md` instead of the local packet doc.

openFindings.title: A small set of non-canonical derived statuses still survives after backfill
openFindings.description: `normalizeDerivedStatus()` canonicalizes whitespace variants but still leaves values such as `done`, `active`, and `in-progress` untouched, which is why six persisted graph-metadata files still sit outside the canonical `complete|in_progress|planned` buckets.
